issue.title: Enhancement: Ctrl+= / Ctrl+- runtime font-size for the live terminal (E3)

phases.title: Add Ctrl+= / Ctrl+- / Ctrl+0 font-size branch to Terminal.tsx's attachCustomKeyEventHandler
verification: Terminal.tsx's attachCustomKeyEventHandler has a new `e.ctrlKey` keydown branch handling font-size before the final `return true`.
Ctrl+= and Ctrl++ (e.key '=' or '+') increase fontSize by 1, clamped to a max of 32, then call fitAddon.fit() and return false.
Ctrl+- (e.key '-') decreases fontSize by 1, clamped to a min of 8, then calls fitAddon.fit() and returns false.
Ctrl+0 resets fontSize to terminalSettings.fontSize, calls fitAddon.fit(), returns false.
e.key === '_' is NOT matched — Ctrl+Shift+- still passes through to the PTY.
No persistence code added; no changes outside Terminal.tsx.
`pnpm build` from the repo root passes (tsc -b + vite build) — no type or unused-symbol errors.
phases.summary: Added a Ctrl+= / Ctrl+- / Ctrl+0 branch to Terminal.tsx's attachCustomKeyEventHandler. Increase matches '=' and '+', decrease matches '-' only (leaving Ctrl+Shift+- / e.key '_' for readline undo), reset restores terminalSettings.fontSize. Each adjusts xterm.options.fontSize (clamped 8–32) and calls fitAddon.fit() to reflow the grid. Runtime-only, no persistence — consistent with the 'applies to new terminals only' settings model. pnpm build clean.
